beats.narration: Every echo on your screen is an answer from the part. Five factors decide how loud that answer comes back.

payload.title: ASK THE ECHO
payload.sub: Five factors set the height of every signal.

beats.narration: Three belong to the flaw: size versus beam and wavelength, type — crack, slag, or pore — and location and tilt.

tiles.s: Where it sits, how it tips

beats.narration: Tilt a planar flaw ten or fifteen degrees off the beam and its echo can collapse. That's why we scan welds from both sides.

payload.heading: Orientation rules everything
payload.note: TILTED 10–15° → ECHO LOST · SCAN BOTH SIDES

beats.narration: Wave and material matter too: higher frequency sees smaller flaws; shear waves roughly halve the wavelength; coarse grain calls for lower frequency, not more gain.
So before you accept or dismiss any signal, ask what size, type, and location could produce it — with your wave, in your material.
